setup.blurb: Connect to Slack for channel browsing, message search, thread context, and team communication.
setup.help_text: Slack's hosted MCP uses OAuth2. On first connection, you'll authorise Sidereal to access your Slack workspace via the browser. No API keys or app creation required.

highlights.text: Channels — browse, read history, list members and pins
Search — find messages across the entire workspace
Threads — read and reply to threaded conversations
Reactions — view and add emoji reactions
Remote MCP — vendor-hosted, no local process needed

readme:
### What it does

Slack gives your AI agent access to your Slack workspace — channels, messages, threads, users, and reactions. This is the official vendor-hosted remote MCP from Slack, so there’s no local process to manage and no API keys to create.

Your agent can browse channels, search messages across the workspace, read thread context, check who’s online, and post messages or replies.

### Remote MCP

Unlike most plugins that run a local process, Slack’s MCP is hosted at `mcp.slack.com`. Authentication uses OAuth2 — on first connection you authorise Sidereal to access your workspace via the browser. No app creation, no API tokens, no local configuration beyond the initial OAuth flow.

### Safety model

Read operations (channels, messages, search, users) work immediately after OAuth authorization.

Write operations (post message, reply to thread, add reaction) require the corresponding OAuth scopes. The OAuth consent screen shows exactly what permissions are being granted before you approve.

### Tool coverage

| Category | Tools | What you can do |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Channels | 4 | List all channels, get info, history, members |
| Messages | 3 | Search, read history, get pins |
| Threads | 1 | Read full thread context |
| Users | 2 | Directory listing, presence status |
| Write | 3 | Post messages, reply to threads, add reactions |

scenarios.label: Catch up on a channel you missed
scenarios.body: Read recent channel history, identify important threads, then read full thread context for any discussions that need your attention.

scenarios.label: Search for a past decision or discussion
scenarios.body: Search messages across the workspace for a topic, review matching results with context, then follow threads to find the full conversation and resolution.

scenarios.label: Post a status update to a project channel
scenarios.body: Check the channel’s recent messages for context, then compose and post a status update. Reply to any follow-up threads with additional details.

notes: First remote MCP in catalog. Vendor-hosted at mcp.slack.com — no local process, no API key. OAuth2 handled by Slack's hosted auth flow. Service mapping is provisional until tools/list is enumerated against the live endpoint. messages-v1 is a partial fit: Slack's channel/thread model is richer than 1:1 messaging.
